vulnerabilities.description: First user to register gets ADMIN role automatically. If the application starts without users or the first registration is exploited, an attacker gains full administrative access.

File upload only validates MIME type (image/jpeg, image/png, etc.) but does not validate file content. Attackers could upload malicious files with fake extensions. Also, uploaded files are served from a public directory which could allow execution if server misconfigured.
File deletion silently ignores errors with empty catch block (`catch {}`). Failed deletions don't return errors to the user, potentially leaving orphaned files or inconsistent state.
No rate limiting on registration endpoint. Attackers could enumerate email addresses and create unauthorized accounts via brute force registration attempts.
Contact update performs delete then insert pattern. If the second operation fails, existing emails/phones are lost with no rollback, leading to potential data loss.

CSV injection protection only handles cells starting with =, +, -, @. Other formula injection vectors (like tab, carriage return via CSV quoting) are not addressed. Consider a more comprehensive sanitization.
Session configuration not visible in provided code. JWT strategy should ensure AUTH_SECRET is properly rotated and not hardcoded. Ensure JWT expiry is set to reasonable values.

Migration script uses $queryRaw with template literal but the values come from already-parsed JSON. While safe in this context, the pattern could be risky if refactored without understanding the security implications.

recommendations: Disable automatic ADMIN promotion for the first user. Instead, require explicit admin setup or use a secure deployment token.
Implement file content validation (magic bytes/mime sniffing) in addition to MIME type checking for uploads.
Move uploaded files outside the public directory or serve them via a protected API endpoint that validates authorization before serving.
Add rate limiting to the registration endpoint using Next.js middleware or a library like rate-limiter-flexible.
Use database transactions for the contact update pattern (delete + insert) to prevent data loss on failure.
Enhance CSV sanitization to handle all formula injection vectors (tabs, newlines, multi-line cells).
Add comprehensive error handling in the photo deletion that surfaces failures instead of silently ignoring them.
Audit all environment variables to ensure no secrets are committed (DATABASE_URL, AUTH_SECRET should come exclusively from environment).
Consider adding account lockout/disable functionality for administrators to revoke compromised accounts.
Add request validation size limits to prevent denial-of-service via large payloads.
